title: Lex Specialis — explicit override paths (Neo4j)
chosen_strategy: Typed relationships (OVERRIDES, MODIFIES, SUPERSEDES) plus node-level Lex metadata from Step 8. Phase 4 queries traverse overrides after retrieval; ranking can combine edge presence, rel.effective_from / effective_to, and node.specificity_rank.

edge_semantics.meaning: The **source** Section’s rule takes precedence over the **target** Section when both apply in the same context (more specific beats more general).
The **source** instrument (e.g. amendment Act) modifies the legal content represented by the **target** Section node.
The **source** instrument replaces or fully supersedes the **target** instrument for the scoped provisions (use rel dates + manifest metadata to scope).

query_hints_for_phase_4: Prefer explicit OVERRIDES/MODIFIES/SUPERSEDES edges when curators encode known conflicts.
When edges are missing, fall back to comparing node.authority_class, node.specificity_rank, and lex_effective_* windows from lex_specialis_v1.
Never treat auto_heuristic edges as ground truth without review_status upgrade.
